Title: Phosphoglycerate dehydrogenase is required for kernel development and defines a predominant serine synthesis pathway in maize

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports protein synthesis and kernel development
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Serine deficiency in developing kernels; 3 mM serine supplementation reverses downstream phosphorylation changes
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activated under serine deficiency/stalled ribosomes to repress general protein translation via eIF2α and S6K signaling
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Strong inference from phospho-eIF2α increase, phospho-S6K decrease, ribosome pausing, and serine rescue; no direct genetic manipulation of GCN2
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The paper proposes maize GCN2 as the amino acid deficiency sensor activated in dek20. 2. Serine deficiency and ribosome stalling are linked to increased eIF2α phosphorylation and decreased S6K phosphorylation. 3. Serine supplementation reverses these signaling outputs, supporting GCN2-pathway involvement.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for modulating translational response to amino acid deficiency, but direct evidence in maize is not provided
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core mechanistic signaling node in the Results/Discussion, but not directly cloned or genetically validated in this study

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Eukaryotic translation initiation factor 2 alpha subunit
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports protein synthesis and kernel development
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Serine deficiency in dek20; 3 mM serine supplementation
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Phosphorylation increases under serine deficiency, contributing to translation initiation inhibition
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Immunoblotting of total and phosphorylated eIF2α with and without serine supplementation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. eIF2α phosphorylation is elevated in dek20 kernels at multiple developmental stages. 2. Increased eIF2α phosphorylation is associated with global translational repression under serine deficiency. 3. Serine supplementation lowers eIF2α phosphorylation.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Marker/target for translational stress response rather than direct metabolic engineering target
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not the primary cloned gene; included because it is a central mechanistic mediator experimentally assayed in Results

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports protein synthesis and kernel development
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Serine deficiency in dek20; 3 mM serine supplementation
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Phosphorylation decreases under serine deficiency, contributing to reduced translation initiation/growth signaling
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Immunoblotting of total and phosphorylated S6K1 with and without serine supplementation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Phosphorylated S6K1 is reduced in dek20 kernels. 2. Reduced S6K1 phosphorylation accompanies serine deficiency and translational inhibition. 3. Serine supplementation increases S6K1 phosphorylation.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential signaling readout for amino acid sufficiency; not directly validated as engineering target in this paper
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mechanistic downstream node measured experimentally, but not directly genetically tested

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Type I phosphoglycerate dehydrogenase; substrate-binding domain, nucleotide-binding domain, regulatory domain
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Phosphorylated serine biosynthesis branch from glycolysis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes 3-phosphoglycerate to 3-phosphohydroxypyruvate with reduction of NAD+ to NADH
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Substrate for protein biosynthesis; supports synthesis of amino acids, nucleotides, phospholipids and sphingolipids; supports kernel development and cell proliferation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Developing kernel, especially starchy endosperm, aleurone layer, and embryo
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms upstream precursor and controls flux into serine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Rate-limiting enzyme established by the paper
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Broadly expressed, with highest expression in developing kernels; prominent in starchy endosperm, aleurone layer, and embryo
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Broadly expressed, with highest expression in developing kernels; prominent in starchy endosperm, aleurone layer, and embryo
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing kernels at 4-22 DAP; in vitro supplementation with 3 mM L-serine or 3 mM L-tyrosine
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Exome sequencing; metabolomics; RNA-seq (CRA019220); Ribo-seq (CRA019221); protein structure data PDB: 9JCM, 9JCN
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes serine biosynthesis and kernel development; loss-of-function dramatically decreases serine and causes embryo lethality, reduced endosperm, and reduced starch and storage proteins
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant cloning and allelism tests with three alleles; metabolite profiling; serine feeding rescue; recombinant enzyme assay; microscale thermophoresis NAD+ binding assay; crystal structure analysis; subcellular localization
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Dek20 encodes maize PGDH1, the plastid-localized rate-limiting enzyme of the phosphorylated serine biosynthesis pathway. 2. dek20 mutations sharply reduce free serine (~80%) and impair kernel development, starch accumulation, and storage protein accumulation. 3. Exogenous serine partially rescues kernel growth, supporting serine deficiency as the causal metabolic defect. 4. The S282L lesion increases NAD+ affinity through His284 and lowers PGDH enzymatic activity, explaining reduced serine biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function causes severe kernel lethality, reduced 100-kernel weight, defective embryo and endosperm, and reduced starch, zein, and non-zein proteins
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for improving serine supply and kernel filling, but complete loss of function is lethal; suitable for dosage tuning or overexpression rather than knockout
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Among four maize PGDH genes, Dek20/PGDH1 is the predominant kernel-expressed member; PGDH2 is very low, PGDH4 undetectable, and other PGDH genes do not compensate in dek20
